scope: backfill-graph-metadata.ts and the description discovery cache write path
summary: Backfill ignores a positional folder arg and defaults to the repo-wide specs root. Description discovery regenerates a global descriptions.json and repairs per-folder files as a search-time side effect.

title: Exact-target backfill mode
problem: A folder passed positionally to backfill is ignored. parseArgs only handles --dry-run, --active-only, --include-archive, --root, so unknown args fall through. With no --root it refreshes the default repo-wide .opencode/specs root.
evidence: backfill-graph-metadata.ts:71-96 parseArgs, :73 default root, :149 walk(root), runBackfill calls collectSpecFolders
proposal: Add --folder or treat one positional path as an exact target that calls refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder for that folder only. Keep repo-wide behavior behind explicit --all.

title: Make backfill collection match writer rules
problem: The collector can walk folders the graph writer rejects, turning one unsupported folder into a fatal traversal stop instead of a skip.
evidence: collectSpecFolders backfill-graph-metadata.ts:115-151, canClassifyAsGraphMetadataPath spec-doc-paths.ts:110-115, refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder throws graph-metadata-parser.ts:1263-1265
proposal: In collectSpecFolders skip candidates whose graph-metadata.json path fails canClassifyAsGraphMetadataPath. Wrap each folder refresh so one corrupt folder reports skipped or failed without aborting the run.

title: Remove write side effects from description discovery
problem: Runtime discovery regenerates a global descriptions.json and repairs per-folder description.json while merely trying to search.
evidence: ensureDescriptionCache writes folder-discovery.ts:1165-1179, generateFolderDescriptions repairs during scan :706-713, hybrid-search.ts:2448-2450
proposal: Make ensureDescriptionCache read-only by default for runtime search and context paths. Return an in-memory cache when stale, write only under an explicit maintenance mode.

title: Canonicalize specFolder independently of caller base
problem: description.json identity depends on the caller basePath, so create.sh and the sk-code recipe produce different specFolder strings.
evidence: generate-description.ts:77-84 path.relative(basePath, folderPath), folder-discovery.ts:883-895, graph normalizes by stripping through specs/ at graph-metadata-parser.ts:653-663
proposal: Resolve description.json.specFolder from the nearest supported specs root, matching graph metadata canonical stripped path. Keep basePath only for containment checks.
